The Vital-Vidhvansak was a pioneering monthly journal that specifically targeted the untouchable community. Gopal Baba Walangkar, a prominent social reformer, published this journal to advocate for the rights and upliftment of the marginalized sections of society. His efforts were crucial in raising awareness and challenging the prevailing caste-based discrimination.

In ancient Indian history, 'kulyavapa' and 'dronavapa' were units of land measurement. These terms were used to quantify agricultural land, with 'kulyavapa' generally referring to a larger area than 'dronavapa'. Their usage indicates the importance of land as a measure of wealth and productivity in the agrarian economy of the time.
